title: The Best Herbal Teas to Combat Laryngitis
intro_desc: Herbal teas for laryngitis are a natural remedy that helps soothe and alleviate the symptoms of laryngitis, an inflammation of the vocal cords. They work by reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting healing. The best herbal teas for laryngitis include Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Thymus vulgaris, Zingiber officinale, Sambucus nigra, Eucalyptus globulus, Verbascum thapsus, Rosmarinus officinalis, Mentha x piperita, and Trifolium pratense. If left untreated, laryngitis can lead to chronic hoarseness, vocal strain, and fatigue, significantly impacting daily life, work, and social interactions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is often used to help soothe a sore throat and calm laryngitis. This is because the plant is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the throat. The tea also contains compounds that have antimicrobial effects, which can help fight off infections that can cause laryngitis. When you drink Echinacea purpurea tea, the anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties can help calm the throat and reduce inflammation, making it easier to swallow and speak. Additionally, the tea can help boost your immune system, which is important for fighting off infections that can cause laryngitis. The soothing and calming effects of the tea can also help you get some rest and feel better, which is important for recovery from laryngitis.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for laryngitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, making it easier to swallow and speak.
Tannins are good for laryngitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe an irritated throat, making it easier to swallow and speak.
Polyphenols are good for laryngitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the throat, allowing for easier breathing and faster healing.

Saponins are good for laryngitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the inflamed tissues in the throat, reducing swelling and pain associated with the condition.

remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for laryngitis
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea angustifolia herbal tea that helps with laryngitis, start by gathering dried roots of the Echinacea plant, also known as Purple Coneflower. You'll need about 1 teaspoon of the dried root for every 8 ounces of water. First, boil fresh water in a pot and then reduce the heat to a simmer. Next, add the dried Echinacea root to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the root's healing properties to infuse into the water. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to catch the roots and discard the solids. Finally, drink the tea warm or at room temperature, ideally 2-3 times a day, to help soothe and calm the irritated throat and larynx associated with laryngitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, is a popular remedy for laryngitis. The tea is made from the root of the licorice plant, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When we have laryngitis, our throat gets inflamed and sore, making it hard to speak. Glycyrrhiza glabra tea helps to reduce this inflammation, making it easier to swallow and talk. The anti-inflammatory compounds in the tea, called glycyrrhizic acid and licoricidin, work together to calm down the throat and bring down the swelling. This allows the throat to heal faster and reduces the pain and discomfort associated with laryngitis. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra tea has antimicrobial properties, which help to fight off any underlying infections that may be causing the laryngitis. Drinking Glycyrrhiza glabra tea several times a day can help to soothe the throat and provide relief from the symptoms of laryngitis.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for laryngitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, allowing the vocal cords to heal and recover more quickly.
Glycyrrhizin is good for laryngitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the swollen and irritated tissues in the throat, reducing pain and discomfort.
Polyphenols are good for laryngitis because they have strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, allowing it to heal faster and feel better.

remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for laryngitis
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea angustifolia herbal tea that helps with laryngitis, start by gathering the dried roots of the Echinacea angustifolia plant. You can purchase these roots from a health food store or online. Next, combine 1-2 teaspoons of the dried roots with 1 cup of boiling water in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid has turned a light brown color. After the steeping time, strain the tea into a separate cup to remove the roots. You can add honey or lemon to the tea if you prefer it sweeter or more flavorful. To use the tea for laryngitis, drink 1-2 cups a day, ideally 3-4 times a day. It is recommended to drink the tea for several days to a week to help soothe and calm the throat and reduce inflammation associated with laryngitis.

Ginger tea also has antibacterial and antiviral properties, which can help fight off infections that can cause laryngitis. Drinking ginger tea can help soothe an irritated throat and reduce pain. Additionally, ginger tea can help thin out mucus, making it easier to cough up and clear out the throat. This can be especially helpful when you have a sore throat and are struggling to clear out mucus and other debris that can irritate the throat.

This herb is known for its soothing properties that can help ease throat pain and inflammation. Begin by boiling water in a kettle or pot, then add one teaspoon of the dried roots for every cup of water you're using. Let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes to allow the active ingredients to infuse into the water. Once the tea has steeped, strain it into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the roots. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to drink the tea while it's still warm. It's recommended to drink 2-3 cups of Echinacea angustifolia tea per day to help alleviate laryngitis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tea, also known as thyme tea, can help soothe and heal laryngitis. Laryngitis is an inflammation of the voice box, causing hoarseness and discomfort when speaking. Thymus vulgaris tea has antiseptic and antibacterial properties that help fight off infections that cause laryngitis. The tea's active compounds, such as thymol, reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that irritate the vocal cords. By drinking thymus vulgaris tea, people with laryngitis can help calm their throats and promote healing. The tea is also soothing and can ease the discomfort of laryngitis, making it easier to speak and swallow. Additionally, thymus vulgaris tea has expectorant properties, which help bring up mucus and phlegm that can contribute to laryngitis. This makes the tea a natural remedy for people looking to alleviate symptoms of laryngitis.

Then, boil water in a pot and pour it over the Echinacea roots. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the roots to release their medicinal properties. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the roots. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to drink it while it's still warm. Drink 2-3 cups of the Echinacea tea a day to help soothe your throat and alleviate laryngitis symptoms. It's essential to note that Echinacea angustifolia tea should not be consumed for extended periods, and it's always best to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tea is a natural remedy that can help with laryngitis. This is because the tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling in the throat. When you have laryngitis, the tissues in your throat become inflamed and irritated, making it hard to speak or swallow. The eucalyptus globulus tea helps to calm down the inflammation and soothe the irritated tissues. This can help to ease the pain and discomfort associated with laryngitis.  The tea also has decongestant properties that can help to clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. This can be especially helpful if you have a cough or sore throat due to laryngitis. Additionally, the tea has antimicrobial properties that can help to fight off any underlying infections that may be contributing to your laryngitis. Drinking eucalyptus globulus tea regularly may also help to reduce the risk of developing laryngitis in the future. To make eucalyptus globulus tea, you can steep dried eucalyptus leaves in hot water for a few minutes. You can then strain the tea and drink it while it's still warm. Some people also add honey or lemon to their tea to make it more palatable.

Rinse the dried roots with clean water to remove any dust or debris. In a medium-sized pot, bring 2 cups of water to a boil. Add 1-2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea angustifolia roots to the boiling water, then reduce the heat to a simmer for 5-7 minutes. Remove the pot from the heat and let it steep for another 10-15 minutes, allowing the roots to release their medicinal properties into the water. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the roots, and discard the solids. Finally, add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe and calm your throat during an episode of laryngitis.

supplementary_best: For laryngitis, a combination of Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Thymus vulgaris is recommended. Echinacea purpurea helps boost the immune system, fighting off the underlying infection that causes laryngitis. Glycyrrhiza glabra soothes the throat, reducing inflammation and irritation, while its anti-inflammatory properties calm the swollen vocal cords. Thymus vulgaris has antibacterial properties, directly targeting and eliminating the bacteria that cause laryngitis. This combination works effectively to tackle the root cause of laryngitis while providing relief from its symptoms.  Note: This combination is based on traditional herbal remedies and has been found to be effective in soothing the throat and fighting off infections. However, it is essential to note that if you have severe laryngitis or persistent symptoms, consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
supplementary_avoid: If you have laryngitis, it's best to avoid teas that can irritate your throat and make symptoms worse. One such herb is Angelica root (Angelica archangelica). The root contains compounds that can dry out mucus and make it harder to cough up, which can be a problem when you already have a sore throat.  Another herb to avoid is Licorice root (Glycyrrhiza uralensis), but it's actually already on your list so we will not mention it.  Instead, let's talk about another herb that is often used for colds, but can be bad for laryngitis: Yerba Santa (Clinopodium douglasii). This herb has a drying effect that can make your throat even drier and more irritated.  Also, avoid teas made from Wild Indigo (Baptisia tinctoria) and Goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis), as they contain compounds that can further irritate your throat and make your symptoms worse.  Lastly, be cautious with teas made from Mullein (Verbascum thapsus) and Slippery Elm (Ulmus rubra) as while they are often used to soothe sore throats, they can be too harsh for laryngitis and make it harder to breathe.
supplementary_other_remedies: When we have laryngitis, our throat gets inflamed and sore, making it hard to talk or even swallow. Besides drinking herbal teas, there are other natural remedies that can help. Honey, for example, is a thick, sticky liquid that's been used for centuries to soothe sore throats. It coats the throat and helps reduce swelling, making it easier to swallow and talk. You can mix it with warm water or lemon juice to make a soothing drink.  Another natural remedy is apple cider vinegar, which has antibacterial properties that can help fight off the infection causing laryngitis. You can mix it with water to make a drink, or use it as a gargle to help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in your throat. Apple cider vinegar is also good for thinning mucus, which can help you cough up mucus and feel better.  Gargling with salt water is another way to help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in your throat. Salt helps to draw out excess fluids and reduce swelling, making it easier to talk and swallow. You can mix 1/4 teaspoon of salt with 8 ounces of warm water and gargle several times a day to help ease your symptoms.  Finally, getting plenty of rest and staying hydrated is also essential for helping your body fight off laryngitis. Drinking plenty of water helps to thin out mucus and keep your throat moist, while getting enough rest allows your body to focus on healing your inflamed throat. By combining these natural remedies with plenty of rest and hydration, you can help ease your laryngitis symptoms and get back to feeling better.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with laryngitis often experience other health issues due to a weakened immune system and inflammation in the throat. One common ailment is bronchitis, an infection of the airways that can cause coughing and difficulty breathing. The herb thyme, rich in antimicrobial properties, can help reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria that cause bronchitis. Another issue is sore throat, which can be soothed by the anti-inflammatory properties of slippery elm. This herb forms a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and discomfort.  Inflammation in the throat can also lead to sinusitis, an infection of the sinuses that causes congestion and pressure in the head. Echinacea, a herb with strong immune-boosting properties, can help reduce inflammation and fight off the underlying infection. Additionally, people with laryngitis may experience a dry, scratchy throat, which can be relieved by the soothing properties of licorice root. This herb has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help calm the throat and reduce irritation.  Some people with laryngitis may also experience a cough, which can be helped by the expectorant properties of marshmallow root. This herb can help bring up mucus and reduce congestion, making it easier to breathe and cough. Another issue is fatigue, which can be addressed by the adaptogenic properties of ashwagandha. This herb can help reduce stress and promote energy, helping to alleviate the exhaustion that often comes with laryngitis.
